prompts.prompt:
Analyze this image thoroughly and generate a challenging, high-quality multiple-choice question suitable for high school or college-level STEM education.
FIRST: Identify the most appropriate subject domain represented in the image: - PHYSICS: If the image shows forces, motion, energy, circuits, waves, etc. - MATHEMATICS: If the image shows geometric figures, graphs, equations, etc. - CHEMISTRY: If the image shows molecular structures, reactions, lab equipment, etc. - BIOLOGY: If the image shows organisms, cells, systems, ecological relationships, etc.
SECOND: Understand and describe for yourself an image and create a high quality (HIGH SCHOOL LEVEL) question that requires critical thinking, application of concepts, or calculations - not just simple identification. DO NOT USE YOUR MEMORY FOR QUESTIONS - CREATE NEW ONES BASED ON THE IMAGE DETAILS!
Format your response as YAML following this EXACT structure, START WITH "- type:": - type: mcq identifier: "MCQ_IMG_{i}" title: "{selected_subject} Concept Application" adaptive: false timeDependent: false prompt: "Based on the diagram, calculate/determine/analyze..." (create a substantial question that requires understanding and application of key concepts) question_text: "Examine the following diagram:" question_image: "__IMAGE_HTML_PLACEHOLDER__" choices: - identifier: "A" text: "A comprehensive, well-reasoned answer option" correct: true - identifier: "B" text: "A plausible but incorrect answer that represents a common misconception" correct: false - identifier: "C" text: "Another plausible but incorrect answer based on partial understanding" correct: false - identifier: "D" text: "A clearly incorrect answer but still relevant to the context" correct: false shuffle: true maxChoices: 1
REQUIREMENTS FOR HIGH-QUALITY QUESTIONS: 1. SOPHISTICATION: Create questions that test higher-order thinking skills (application, analysis, evaluation) rather than mere recall or identification 2. SPECIFICITY: Reference specific elements from the image with precise values if shown. CONSIDER QUESTION/IMAGE SPECIFIC DETAIL/REQUIREMENT: {img_prompt} 3. CHALLENGE: Include calculations, concept applications, or analytical reasoning 4. BELIEVABILITY: All answer options should be plausible to someone with partial understanding 5. DISCRIMINATION: Design answers that distinguish between different levels of understanding 6. CONTEXT: Provide enough context in the prompt to fully understand what's being asked
SUBJECT-SPECIFIC GUIDANCE: - PHYSICS: Include questions about forces, equilibrium, motion analysis, energy transformations, or circuit analysis. FAVOR CALCULATIONS FIRST WITH CRITICAL THINKING! - MATHEMATICS: Focus on geometric proofs, algebraic relationships, trigonometric applications, or coordinate geometry analysis. FAVOR CALCULATIONS FIRST WITH CRITICAL THINKING AND APPLICATIONS! - CHEMISTRY: Create questions about molecular structures, reaction mechanisms, stoichiometry, or thermodynamic principles. FAVOR CALCULATIONS FIRST WITH CRITICAL THINKING, THEN APPLICATIONS AND MECHANISM OF PROCESS! - BIOLOGY: Emphasize system relationships, functional analysis, or process explanations rather than simple identification. FAVOR CRITICAL THINKING, MECHANISM OF PROCESS, APPLICATIONS!
CRITICAL TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS: 1. identifier - Must be "MCQ_IMG_{i}" 2. title - Include the subject area in the title 3. adaptive - Must be 'false' 4. timeDependent - Must be 'false' 5. prompt - Must contain a substantial, challenging question 6. choices - Exactly 4 choices with identifiers A, B, C, D 7. shuffle - Set to 'true' 8. maxChoices - Set to 1 9. DO NOT USE LaTeX FOR FORMULAS. USE INLINE EQUATION FORMAT FOR MATH RELATED INFORMATION AND EASY TO MOVE TO RICH TEXT EDITOR OVERALL.
Your response must be VALID YAML only with no additional text or explanations.

Generate a true/false question based on this image.
Format your response as YAML following this structure, START WITH "- type:": - type: tf identifier: "TF_IMG_{i}" title: "Image-based True/False Question" adaptive: false timeDependent: false question_text: "<Here should be comprehensive, meaningful and educational question text based on the attached image (if available) and related subject>" question_image: "__IMAGE_HTML_PLACEHOLDER__" prompt: "Is this statement true or false: [statement about the image and question text]?" correct: true (or false, depending on your statement) shuffle: false
IMPORTANT: 1. Create a clear statement about the image that is either definitely true or false 2. Ensure the statement is educational and relevant to what's visible
Your response should be YAML only with no additional text or explanations.

Generate a fill-in-the-blank question based on this image. Format your response as YAML following this structure: - type: fib identifier: "FIB_IMG_{i}" title: "Image-based Fill in Blank Question" adaptive: false timeDependent: false question_text: "" question_image: '

' prompt: "The question is only for format purposes. Generate a nice, educational question to evaluate knowledge based on the question. Complete the following statement: The object shown in the image is a _." correctAnswers: - ["correct answer", "alternative correct answer"] expectedLength: 10 IMPORTANT: 1. Create a statement with a blank (marked by _) about the image 2. Provide all possible correct answers within the array 3. Make sure the question is clear and educational 4.DO NOT USE LaTeX FOR FORMULAS. USE INLINE EQUATION FORMAT FOR MATH RELATED INFORMATION AND EASY TO MOVE TO RICH TEXT EDITOR OVERALL. Your response should be YAML only with no additional text or explanations.

Generate a matching question based on this image.
Format your response as YAML following this structure, START WITH "- type:": - type: match identifier: "MATCH_IMG_{i}" title: "Image-based Matching Question" adaptive: false timeDependent: false question_text: "Examine the following image:" question_image: "__IMAGE_HTML_PLACEHOLDER__" prompt: "Match the items on the left with their corresponding descriptions on the right." matchSets: source: - identifier: "s1" text: "First source item" matchMax: 1 - identifier: "s2" text: "Second source item" matchMax: 1 - identifier: "s3" text: "Third source item" matchMax: 1 target: - identifier: "t1" text: "First target description" matchMax: 1 - identifier: "t2" text: "Second target description" matchMax: 1 - identifier: "t3" text: "Third target description" matchMax: 1 correctPairs: - ["s1", "t1"] - ["s2", "t2"] - ["s3", "t3"] shuffle: true
IMPORTANT: 1. Create matching pairs based on elements visible in the image 2. Make sure the matches are clear and educational 3. Provide at least 3 source-target pairs
Your response should be YAML only with no additional text or explanations.
